basics.name: Avitesh Kesharwani
basics.title: Enterprise Architecture & Engineering Excellence Leader
basics.subtitle: IEEE Senior Member | Globee® Awards Judge 2025 | Sr. Principal Consultant | 10+ Years in Cloud, AI & Financial Services
basics.summary: Enterprise Architecture and Engineering Excellence Leader with 10+ years driving technical productivity, delivery standards, and modernization across regulated financial services. Proven track record of scaling CI/CD adoption, test automation maturity, and engineering best practices across 50+ applications and 15+ teams. Combines deep hands-on architecture experience with a passion for coaching — having led 12+ intensive training programs, mentored senior engineers and architects, and evangelized TDD, BDD, and Shift-Left practices at enterprise scale.
basics.heroSummary: 10+ years architecting enterprise-scale cloud solutions. Leading AI-driven underwriting automation at Ryan Specialty Group — reducing quote turnaround by 35%, improving risk scoring accuracy by 28% through RAG, AKS migrations, and predictive analytics.

bullets: Led solution architecture and target-state design for AI-driven underwriting automation platforms, aligning business risk models with scalable cloud-native architectures; reduced quote turnaround time by 35% while improving risk scoring accuracy by 28%

Architected underwriting solutions designed to integrate with commercial P&C insurance platforms (e.g., Duck Creek / Guidewire ecosystems), defining API and event-driven integration patterns aligned with enterprise architecture and regulatory standards

Led adoption of Playwright-based test automation across multiple underwriting applications, establishing shift-left testing practices and automated regression suites that accelerated release confidence and reduced manual QA cycles
Ensured architectural resiliency through high-availability design, disaster recovery strategies, and business continuity planning in regulated banking environments

Defined end-state architecture and modernization roadmap for specialty insurance underwriting systems, migrating legacy IIS-based platforms to Azure AKS, ensuring alignment with enterprise standards, security controls, and regulatory requirements
Acted as primary architecture liaison between underwriting leadership, product owners, and engineering teams — translating business priorities into architecture blueprints and integration patterns

Governed architecture design and engineering standards across multi-cloud ecosystems (AWS, Azure, GCP), driving CI/CD pipeline maturity and ensuring non-functional requirements (availability, scalability, security) with 99.9% uptime

Provided architectural oversight and hands-on technical coaching throughout delivery lifecycle, ensuring design integrity, code quality standards, and engineering best practices from concept through production

experience.role: Sr. Manager, Cloud Center for Enablement
bullets: Defined enterprise architecture principles, engineering quality standards, and reference architectures across a portfolio of 50+ applications, driving technical debt identification and systematic remediation
Led architecture governance and automation maturity assessments through structured review processes, ensuring alignment with CI/CD standards, security, compliance, and risk controls
Developed future-state blueprints and target operating models aligning IT portfolios to business capabilities
Advised senior leadership on cloud modernization strategy, investment prioritization, and enterprise technology roadmaps
Defined and governed enterprise cloud reference architectures and solution standards across AWS and Azure, supporting large-scale underwriting and banking platforms in highly regulated environments

Established Transformation Hub for cloud-native migration using 15-Factor methodologies; championed TDD/BDD practices, shift-left testing adoption, and led Spring Boot migrations with complete CI/CD integration
Partnered with business and technology leaders to shape roadmaps and future-state architectures, balancing modernization goals with regulatory and operational constraints
Provided hands-on technical coaching to senior engineers and architects across 15+ teams, reinforcing TDD/BDD practices, shift-left testing, architecture governance, and engineering quality standards
Spearheaded migration from Adobe ColdFusion legacy portal to microservices architecture using Python/Django and VueJS, improving performance by 70% and serving 5,000+ internal users
Established Transformation Hub for cloud-native migration using 15-Factor methodologies; championed TDD practices and led Spring Boot migrations with complete CI/CD integration

Designed and delivered 12 intensive 80-hour technical training programs on Cloud Native, CI/CD, and TDD practices, upskilling 100+ engineers across 4 states — acting as a player-coach bridging methodology with hands-on execution
Implemented JIRA Service Desk API middleware for automated ticket creation, reducing manual effort by 550 hours annually
Facilitated 6 'Roadmap to Enterprise Agile' workshops for Senior Management across four states
Designed and implemented AWS-based solutions using S3, Lambda, ECS Fargate, SQS, SNS, and API Gateway for secure, scalable applications
Developed enterprise document management system (EDM) using EMC Documentum, managing mission-critical shipment documents with automated retention policies and legal holds
Architected Online Revenue System using Java, Spring MVC, and SOAP Web Services with Oracle Database, optimizing FedEx revenue operations
Automated retention services reducing manual effort by 550 hours; engineered middleware for document conversion independence
Led code reviews and Agile ceremonies; functioned as Onsite Coordinator bridging offshore team communication
Progressed through roles: Software Engineer (Nov 2013 – Mar 2017, Pune) → Associate Consultant (Apr 2017 – Mar 2018, Pune) → Consultant (Apr 2018 – Sep 2018, Orlando)

domainSkills.description: Commercial & specialty underwriting platforms, risk intake, rating, quoting, policy lifecycle integrations, regulatory and non-functional requirements; Duck Creek / Guidewire ecosystem awareness.
Enterprise architecture, application portfolio modernization, security and risk controls, and platform governance in highly regulated environments (Wells Fargo).
Revenue and document management platforms, high-volume, transaction-intensive systems, and compliance-driven operational workflows (FedEx).
CI/CD maturity assessments, test automation strategy (Playwright, SonarQube), technical debt management, engineering standards governance, and large-scale developer enablement programs.

items: Azure (AKS, App Service, Key Vault, VMs)
AWS (Lambda, EC2, S3, RDS, ECS Fargate, SQS, SNS)
